author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add support for parsing more assembly (#2027)
message_body:
I want to be able to handle the output of gcc/clang on our C code.

Right now, I have added support for parsing the assembly output.
Equivalence checking is still to come.

Also still to come is parsing data declarations

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add more operations to parsing in the equivalence checker (#1197)
message_body:
We now support parsing `call`, `cmovb`, `cmp`, `db`, `dd`, `dq`, `dw`,
`je`, `jmp`, `mul`, `pop`, `push`, `rcr`, and `shrx`, and prefixes like
`rep ret`, `repz ret`, `repnz ret`.  We also support labels, `align`,
and `default rel`.  We should probably try to properly support a larger
fraction of https://www.nasm.us/xdoc/2.15.05/html/nasmdoc3.html at some
point.

We now parse instructions (opcodes) in a case-insensitive manner, though
many other places are still case-sensitive.

The stack is now computed based on push and pop instructions as well as
the adjustments to rsp, and supports aggregating multiple constant-sized
adjustments to rsp.

We don't yet support the `0F3h` syntax alternative for the hex `0xF3`,
mostly because I couldn't be bothered to write a parser for it right
now.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add some basic parsing for assembly
message_body:
Also add examples from assembly files via
```bash
for i in *.asm; do (echo 'Require Import Coq.Strings.String Coq.Lists.List.'; echo 'Import ListNotations.'; echo 'Local Open Scope string_scope.'; echo 'Local Open Scope list_scope.'; echo 'Example example : list string := ['; (cat $i | grep -v "^;$(for i in $(seq 100); do echo -n .; done)" | sed 's/"/""/g; s/^\(.*\)/"\1";/g'); echo '""].') > src/Assembly/Parse/Examples/${i%.*}.v; git add  src/Assembly/Parse/Examples/${i%.*}.v; done
```

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Generalized sub/opp balance for unsaturated solinas
message_body:
Alternative to #857.

Rather than redistributing an existing encoding, this version simply
encodes a number using `partition` that, when added to the relevant
bounds on a limb-by-limb basis without carrying, gives something which
evaluates to the desired result.

The limb-by-limb addition ensures the bounds we want, and the encoding
scheme ensures the correct value.  Furthermore, we don't need to know
ahead-of-time how many copies of the prime we need to add. This is
pleasingly simple to me.

Additionally, as in #857, the tweak to make `to_bytes` work for p224
(and other primes with low limbs) and unsaturated solinas.  The tweak
here is a bit uglier than in #857, because balancing here doesn't
guarantee the same value under `eval`, only something congruent to that
value.  So we need to ensure that we're encoding at least one copy of
the prime.

author: jadep
message_subject: nicer specs and naming, straightline_call now super slow but proofs in ladderstep work eventually

author: jadep
message_subject: Add first sketches of an API for bedrock2 synthesis

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Bump rewriter - Allow specifying more rewriter options
message_body:
We can now more easily disable use of pre-reduced constants, and can also more easily disable use of the decision tree. Unfortunately the extra arguments make things a bit slower in PushButtonSynthesis.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Rearrange ordering of perf data to get more data that we care about

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
These are the test scripts used for `make perf` and `make perf-csv` used
to generate the data for the 2020 POPL rewriting paper submission.
